data.content:
""" "
Objective: Act as a Business Analyst of PRODUCT_NAME implementation, classify the given stories within existing epics or generate new epics.

Instructions to identify epics?
1. Go through the given stories one by one
For each story
2. Classify them into existing epics if possible. Provide the PE_ID of the existing epic. 
3. If none of the existing epics can fit the story, then create a new epic. Say "new" for the PE_ID in the output. Atleast 3 stories should be part of a new epic. Otherwise, accomodate in the others.
Note - "linked metadata object names" contains the list of all Metadata/Customization Object Name of the stories as a list of strings that are linked to this epic.


### Input Requirements

1. PRODUCT_NAME
   - Name of the software ecosystem

2. Existing Epics:  
   - Description: List of epics already in the system
   - Format: A text block

3. SEQUENCE STEP TABLE:  
   - Description: A table that captures the business process steps which becomes the scope of the phases and epics to be generated.
   - Format: A JSON object

---

#### ** Output Structure**
   - **Block-Based Output**:  
     Generate output as blocks. Each block should include:  
       - **ID**: A unique identifier for the block.  
       - **Path**: The hierarchical location within the JSON structure, starting with `root`.  
       - **JSON Content**: Include detailed information about components and their relationships.

    - Avoid Extraneous Content:
        - Include only the necessary fields in each block, omitting any extra text or explanations.


```
<block>
ID: root_0
Path: root.epics[0]
{
    "PE_ID": "new",
    "Epic": "<Name of the new epic>",
    "Epic Criteria": "<The acceptance criteria for the epic>",
    "linked metadata object names":[<Metadata/Customization Object Name>, ...]
}
</block>

<block>
ID: root_1
Path: root.epics[1]
{
    "PE_ID": "<PE_ID> of the existing epic from the input",
    "Epic": "<Name of the existing epic>",
    "Epic Criteria": "<The acceptance criteria for the epic>",
    "linked metadata object names":[<Metadata/Customization Object Name>, ...]
}
</block>
```

---

### **Path for Lists**
To accommodate lists in the JSON structure, include indices for list items as follows:  
**Example**: `Path: root.epics[0]`

---

### **Output Validation**
1. Ensure each block has a valid JSON structure.  
2. Validate the hierarchy and paths to ensure all details are captured accurately.  
3. Verify that the output is exhaustive and no critical information is omitted.  

---

"""

Input:

1) PRODUCT_NAME
Salesforce

2) Existing Epics with system gen epic id(PE_ID) and their details
[]

3) The Stories are::
[{"Module Name": "Student Enrolment Process: Expression of Interest (EOI)", "Business Process Name": "EOI Abandonment & Auto-save Resume", "Sequence Step Name": "Open Public EOI Form", "Initiator Type": "Persona", "Initiator Name": "Student", "Executor Type": "Container", "Executor Name": "Salesforce Sites", "Container Name": "Student", "Component Name": "Lightning Web Component (LWC)", "Metadata Class Name": "LightningComponentBundle", "Metadata/Customization Object Name": "LWC_Public_EOI", "Description": "Deploy a two-page responsive LWC bundle hosted on Salesforce Sites that renders the public EOI form, consumes picklist metadata, performs client hydration and calls autosave and resume endpoints. Used by anonymous students on Sites; maintained by Site Admin and System Administrator.", "Requirement Rows": [{"Requirement ID": "F20", "Requirement Description": "Automatically persist partial EOI form data at configurable intervals (e.g., every 30 seconds or on field change) from the public LWC into a draft Lead record to prevent data loss for anonymous or authenticated users. When I am filling the EOI form and want my progress saved automatically so I don"t lose entered information if I close my browser, then The LWC triggers an Apex REST upsert that saves the partial payload to a Lead record (Draft flag = true) and returns a draftId and resume token; the UI shows "Draft saved" confirmation.", "req_id": "F20", "requirement_description": "Automatically persist partial EOI form data at configurable intervals (e.g., every 30 seconds or on field change) from the public LWC into a draft Lead record to prevent data loss for anonymous or authenticated users. When I am filling the EOI form and want my progress saved automatically so I don"t lose entered information if I close my browser, then The LWC triggers an Apex REST upsert that saves the partial payload to a Lead record (Draft flag = true) and returns a draftId and resume token; the UI shows "Draft saved" confirmation.", "req_type": "Functional", "requirement_title": "Autosave Drafts", "Requirement Title": "Autosave Drafts"}, {"Requirement ID": "F21", "Requirement Description": "Store all autosaved fields (including optional fields) on the Lead object per the Hybrid approach; include a Draft_Metadata__c section and Token__c field to enable resume functionality and linkage to Contact/Account later. When I want the system to save all fields I enter (including optional ones) so they can be resumed later, then system upserts the Lead record with all supplied fields, sets Draft__c = true, stores Token__c (secure token), and populates Draft_Metadata__c for audit and resume reference.", "req_id": "F21", "requirement_description": "Store all autosaved fields (including optional fields) on the Lead object per the Hybrid approach; include a Draft_Metadata__c section and Token__c field to enable resume functionality and linkage to Contact/Account later. When I want the system to save all fields I enter (including optional ones) so they can be resumed later, then system upserts the Lead record with all supplied fields, sets Draft__c = true, stores Token__c (secure token), and populates Draft_Metadata__c for audit and resume reference.", "req_type": "Functional", "requirement_title": "Draft Persistence", "Requirement Title": "Draft Persistence"}, {"Requirement ID": "F22", "Requirement Description": "Generate a secure, time-limited resume token (Token__c) for each draft Lead; the token is encoded in a resume link sent by SMS and stored with expiry metadata on the Lead.
